Title: Select an order set and view its member orders before applying
Preconditions: Logged-in clinician with ordering privileges; patient has an active visit; at least one published order set exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit | 2. Open CPOE order entry | 3. Search and select the "Adult Community-Acquired Pneumonia" order set | 4. Review the member orders listed in the preview panel
Expected_Result: Order set expands to show all member orders (labs, drugs, radiology) with default values; nothing is committed until signed

Title: Filter order sets by clinical specialty and care setting
Preconditions: Multiple order sets exist tagged by specialty and care setting (inpatient/outpatient)
Test_Steps: 1. Open order set catalog | 2. Apply specialty filter "Cardiology" | 3. Apply care setting filter "Inpatient" | 4. Observe filtered list
Expected_Result: Only order sets matching both filters appear; counts update; clearing filters restores full list

Title: Attempt to select a retired/unpublished order set
Preconditions: An order set exists in retired state
Test_Steps: 1. Open order set catalog | 2. Search by the retired order set name | 3. Attempt to select it
Expected_Result: Retired order set does not appear in selectable catalog, or is shown disabled with a retired indicator and cannot be applied

Title: Order set search returns no results for unknown term
Preconditions: Order set catalog accessible
Test_Steps: 1. Open order set catalog | 2. Search for nonexistent term "zzzqqq"
Expected_Result: Empty-state message is shown; no error; search box remains usable

Title: Apply order set without an active visit/care setting
Preconditions: Patient has no active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with no active visit | 2. Attempt to apply an inpatient order set
Expected_Result: System blocks application and prompts to start a visit/select care setting; no orders are created

Title: Apply a protocol that auto-populates timed and recurring orders
Preconditions: Sepsis protocol order set is published with timed members (e.g., lactate at 0h and 6h)
Test_Steps: 1. Select "Sepsis 1-Hour Bundle" protocol | 2. Review auto-generated schedule | 3. Sign the protocol
Expected_Result: All protocol orders are created with correct scheduling (repeat lactate, fluids, antibiotics); each carries protocol provenance reference

Title: Protocol orders inherit ordering provider and visit context
Preconditions: Clinician signed in; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Apply a protocol | 2. Sign | 3. Open each generated order's detail
Expected_Result: Every generated order shows the signing provider, encounter/visit linkage, and correct care setting

Title: Weight-based protocol dosing computes correct drug quantities
Preconditions: Pediatric weight-based protocol exists; patient weight recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open pediatric patient with weight obs | 2. Apply weight-based protocol | 3. Review computed doses
Expected_Result: Doses are calculated as mg/kg or mL/kg using recorded weight; rounding follows protocol rules; values displayed before sign

Title: Weight-based protocol blocks when weight is missing
Preconditions: Pediatric weight-based protocol; patient has no weight recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient without weight | 2. Apply weight-based protocol | 3. Attempt to compute/sign
Expected_Result: System prompts for weight before dose computation; signing is blocked until weight provided; no zero/null doses created

Title: Conditional member shown only when its trigger condition is true
Preconditions: Order set with conditional member: "Insulin sliding scale IF glucose > 250"
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set with patient glucose=300 | 2. Observe conditional member | 3. Repeat with glucose=120
Expected_Result: With glucose=300 the conditional insulin order appears/auto-selects; with glucose=120 it is hidden or unchecked

Title: Decision table for renal-adjusted dosing across eGFR bands
Preconditions: Order set with renal dose adjustment rules keyed on eGFR
Test_Steps: 1. Set eGFR per row | 2. Apply order set | 3. Verify chosen dose band
Test_Data: eGFR rows: >=60 full dose; 30-59 75%; 15-29 50%; <15 hold + alert
Expected_Result: Each eGFR band maps to the correct dose tier; <15 holds the drug and raises an alert; boundaries 60/30/15 resolve to the documented band

Title: Conditional logic boundary at exact threshold value
Preconditions: Conditional rule "order potassium IF K+ < 3.5"
Test_Steps: 1. Apply with K+ = 3.5 | 2. Apply with K+ = 3.4 | 3. Apply with K+ = 3.6
Expected_Result: K+=3.4 triggers the order; 3.5 and 3.6 do not (strict less-than); behavior is consistent and documented

Title: Conditional order with missing dependent observation
Preconditions: Conditional rule depends on an observation not yet recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set where trigger obs is absent | 2. Observe handling of the conditional member
Expected_Result: Conditional member is shown as indeterminate/requires-input rather than silently included or excluded; clinician is prompted

Title: Edit dose, frequency and route of a member order before signing
Preconditions: Order set selected, not yet signed
Test_Steps: 1. Expand a drug member | 2. Change dose 500mg->250mg | 3. Change frequency BID->TID | 4. Change route PO->IV | 5. Review
Expected_Result: Edited values persist in the draft and are reflected at sign; defaults are overridden cleanly

Title: Deselect optional members and keep mandatory members
Preconditions: Order set with mandatory and optional members
Test_Steps: 1. Open expanded order set | 2. Uncheck two optional members | 3. Attempt to uncheck a mandatory member
Expected_Result: Optional members can be removed; mandatory members are locked/cannot be deselected and are clearly marked

Title: Override a member dose beyond max allowed triggers validation
Preconditions: Drug member has a defined max single dose
Test_Steps: 1. Edit member dose to exceed max | 2. Attempt to sign
Expected_Result: Validation blocks or hard-warns on the over-max dose; clinician must acknowledge/justify or correct before sign

Title: Boundary on editable quantity/duration fields
Preconditions: Member order with numeric duration field
Test_Steps: 1. Set duration=0 | 2. Set duration=negative | 3. Set duration at max allowed | 4. Set duration just above max
Expected_Result: 0 and negative are rejected; max accepted; above-max rejected with a clear message

Title: Add an ad-hoc order to the draft alongside order set members
Preconditions: Order set draft open
Test_Steps: 1. Add a free-standing order not in the set | 2. Confirm it joins the draft | 3. Sign all
Expected_Result: Ad-hoc order is added to the same signing batch and committed with the set; provenance distinguishes set vs ad-hoc

Title: Discard draft order set without signing
Preconditions: Order set draft with edits exists
Test_Steps: 1. Make edits | 2. Click Cancel/Discard | 3. Confirm discard | 4. Re-open order entry
Expected_Result: Draft is discarded; no orders persisted; patient order list unchanged; re-opening starts clean

Title: Sign all selected order set members in a single action
Preconditions: Order set draft reviewed; clinician has signing authority
Test_Steps: 1. Review the full draft | 2. Click Sign All | 3. Authenticate if prompted | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: All members transition to active/signed atomically; one signing event recorded; orders appear in active orders list

Title: Partial bulk sign when one member fails validation
Preconditions: Draft contains one member that fails validation (e.g., over-max dose)
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt Sign All | 2. Observe outcome for valid vs invalid members
Expected_Result: Either the whole batch is blocked atomically with the offending member flagged, or valid members commit and invalid ones are retained as draft with clear status; no partial silent loss

Title: Bulk sign requires re-authentication / e-signature
Preconditions: E-signature/re-auth policy enabled for order signing
Test_Steps: 1. Click Sign All | 2. Enter wrong password | 3. Enter correct password
Expected_Result: Wrong password blocks signing; correct password completes; signing event records signer identity and timestamp

Title: User without signing privilege cannot bulk sign
Preconditions: User role lacks order-signing privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as restricted user | 2. Build order set draft | 3. Attempt Sign All
Expected_Result: Sign action is disabled/denied with authorization error; no orders are activated; attempt is audited

Title: Concurrent bulk sign of the same draft by two sessions
Preconditions: Same draft open in two sessions
Test_Steps: 1. Session A signs the draft | 2. Session B attempts to sign the same draft
Expected_Result: Second sign is rejected or recognizes already-signed state; no duplicate orders are created; optimistic-lock/stale message shown

Title: Drug-drug interaction alert fires on an order set member
Preconditions: DDI rules active; patient on warfarin; order set adds an interacting drug
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set adding an NSAID | 2. Observe interaction alert before sign
Expected_Result: DDI alert displays severity and interacting pair; clinician must acknowledge/override-with-reason before signing that member

Title: Drug-allergy alert blocks/warns on order set member
Preconditions: Patient has documented penicillin allergy; order set includes amoxicillin
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set | 2. Observe drug-allergy alert | 3. Attempt override
Expected_Result: Drug-allergy alert appears with reaction details; override requires a reason; event audited

Title: Duplicate-therapy alert when order set duplicates an active order
Preconditions: Patient already has active order for a drug in the set
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set containing the same drug class | 2. Observe duplicate alert
Expected_Result: Duplicate/therapeutic-overlap alert is raised; clinician chooses to keep, replace, or cancel the duplicate

Title: Override reason is captured and audited for safety alerts
Preconditions: A safety alert fires that requires override reason
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a DDI alert | 2. Override with reason "benefit outweighs risk" | 3. Sign | 4. Open audit log
Expected_Result: Override reason, alert type, severity, user and timestamp are persisted in the audit trail and retrievable

Title: Pairwise alert-handling across alert type and user action
Preconditions: Alert engine supports multiple alert types and actions
Test_Steps: 1. Generate each alert type | 2. Apply each user action | 3. Verify outcome
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination yields the correct outcome: accept proceeds, override requires reason, cancel removes the member; no combination crashes or silently passes

Title: Hard-stop alert prevents signing without resolution
Preconditions: A hard-stop (non-overridable) rule is configured (e.g., absolute contraindication)
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set triggering hard-stop | 2. Attempt to override | 3. Attempt to sign
Expected_Result: Hard-stop cannot be overridden; the offending member cannot be signed; clinician must remove/modify it to proceed

Title: Non-formulary drug in order set is flagged at selection
Preconditions: Drug formulary configured; order set contains a non-formulary drug
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set | 2. Observe formulary status on each drug member
Expected_Result: Non-formulary member is flagged with a formulary indicator; justification or substitution is requested before sign

Title: Therapeutic substitution suggested for non-formulary drug
Preconditions: Formulary substitution mapping exists
Test_Steps: 1. Select non-formulary drug member | 2. Open substitution suggestion | 3. Accept formulary alternative
Expected_Result: System suggests the formulary equivalent; accepting replaces the member while preserving dose intent; provenance notes substitution

Title: Restricted/antimicrobial-stewardship drug requires approval
Preconditions: Drug flagged as restricted requiring stewardship approval
Test_Steps: 1. Apply order set with restricted antibiotic | 2. Attempt to sign without approval
Expected_Result: Member requires stewardship approval/role; signing without approval is blocked or routes for approval; action audited

Title: Decision table for formulary status by care setting and insurance
Preconditions: Formulary varies by care setting and payer
Test_Steps: 1. Vary care setting and payer per row | 2. Check drug formulary status
Expected_Result: Each combination resolves to the documented formulary status and resulting prompt; defaults applied where mapping absent

Title: Create orders from an order set via REST and verify persistence
Preconditions: REST API access with ordering privileges; valid session token
Test_Steps: 1. POST order set application to the orders REST endpoint | 2. GET the created orders | 3. Validate fields
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with created order resources; GET confirms members persisted with correct orderer, encounter and care setting

Title: Reject malformed order set payload with 400
Preconditions: REST API access
Test_Steps: 1. POST payload missing required orderer | 2. POST payload with invalid concept UUID
Expected_Result: API returns 400/422 with a descriptive validation error; no partial orders created

Title: Unauthorized order set creation is rejected
Preconditions: API reachable without/with insufficient token
Test_Steps: 1. POST order set with no auth token | 2. POST with token lacking order privilege
Expected_Result: Requests return 401/403; no orders created; attempts are logged

Title: Order set definition maps to FHIR PlanDefinition with actions
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint exposes plan definitions; order set published
Test_Steps: 1. GET PlanDefinition for the order set | 2. Validate action elements and references
Expected_Result: PlanDefinition returned with action items referencing ActivityDefinition; conditions and dynamicValues represented; resource validates against the profile

Title: Applying order set produces FHIR RequestGroup / order resources
Preconditions: FHIR $apply supported or RequestGroup generation available
Test_Steps: 1. Invoke PlanDefinition $apply for the patient | 2. Inspect resulting RequestGroup and referenced MedicationRequest/ServiceRequest
Expected_Result: A RequestGroup is returned linking generated MedicationRequest/ServiceRequest resources; each carries subject, authoredOn and requester; references resolve

Title: FHIR order resources reference correct code systems
Preconditions: Generated FHIR orders available
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve a generated MedicationRequest | 2. Inspect medication coding system | 3. Retrieve a ServiceRequest and inspect coding
Expected_Result: Medication codes use RxNorm system URI; lab/service codes use LOINC; system URIs are correct and codes are valid

Title: Signed order set emits HL7 v2 ORM/OMP messages per member
Preconditions: HL7 v2 outbound interface configured for orders
Test_Steps: 1. Sign an order set | 2. Capture outbound HL7 messages | 3. Parse ORC/OBR/RXO segments
Expected_Result: One order message per member is emitted with correct ORC placer order numbers, OBR/RXO details and patient/visit identifiers

Title: Order cancellation from set emits HL7 cancel control code
Preconditions: HL7 outbound configured; an active order from a set exists
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel a signed member order | 2. Capture outbound HL7 | 3. Inspect ORC-1 control code
Expected_Result: Cancellation message uses the correct ORC-1 control code referencing the original placer order number

Title: Order set application is atomic and rolls back on failure
Preconditions: DB access to verify transaction; ability to inject a mid-batch failure
Test_Steps: 1. Apply and sign a multi-member set | 2. Force a failure on the last member | 3. Inspect orders table
Expected_Result: Either all members commit or none do; no orphan/partial rows; transaction rolls back cleanly on failure

Title: Each order persists provenance link to its source order set
Preconditions: Orders created from a set; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Apply and sign an order set | 2. Query order rows | 3. Verify source order set reference column/linkage
Expected_Result: Each generated order row carries a reference to the originating order set/protocol; query returns the linkage for reporting

Title: Order set selection and sign flow is keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Order entry UI loaded; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate catalog with Tab/arrows | 2. Expand a set with Enter/Space | 3. Toggle members | 4. Reach and activate Sign All
Expected_Result: All interactive elements are reachable and operable by keyboard with a visible focus indicator; no keyboard trap; logical tab order

Title: Safety alerts are announced to screen readers
Preconditions: Screen reader active; alert-triggering set available
Test_Steps: 1. Apply a set that triggers a DDI alert | 2. Listen for the alert via screen reader
Expected_Result: The alert is exposed as an ARIA alert/live region and announced with its severity and message; override controls are labeled

Title: Explore order set behavior with rapid edits and toggling
Preconditions: Order set draft open
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly toggle members on/off | 2. Edit then revert fields | 3. Add/remove ad-hoc orders quickly | 4. Attempt sign mid-edit
Expected_Result: No stale/ghost members; computed values and alerts stay consistent with current selections; sign reflects the final visible state

Title: Explore order set versioning when an updated version publishes mid-draft
Preconditions: An order set has a draft open while a new version is published
Test_Steps: 1. Open a draft from order set v1 | 2. Publish v2 with changed defaults | 3. Continue and sign the v1 draft
Expected_Result: System either pins the draft to v1 with a version note or warns of the new version; signed orders record which version was used; no silent mixing

Title: Order lifecycle from draft to signed to discontinued
Preconditions: Member order signed and active
Test_Steps: 1. Verify state=Active after sign | 2. Discontinue the order with reason | 3. Verify state=Discontinued | 4. Attempt to edit discontinued order
Expected_Result: State transitions occur in order; discontinue requires reason; discontinued orders are immutable; invalid transitions rejected

Title: Pairwise across care setting, role and order set type
Preconditions: Multiple care settings, roles and order set types configured
Test_Steps: 1. For each pair apply an order set | 2. Verify permission and applicability
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination enforces correct permissions and applicability; disallowed combinations are blocked with clear messaging
